title: Busy Periods
description:
You are given a list of employee shift intervals. Each interval is [start, end) and uses integer timestamps.

Return the merged time periods during which at least k employees are on duty simultaneously.

Rules:
- Intervals are half-open: a shift ending at time t does not cover t.
- The returned intervals must be sorted, non-overlapping, and also half-open.
- Zero-length intervals ([t, t)) do not contribute coverage.
- If k <= 0, raise ValueError.
- The input list may be unsorted and may contain overlapping or adjacent intervals.
function_signature: def busy_periods(intervals: list[list[int]], k: int) -> list[list[int]]:
